# Kerb

> Kerb (kerb.autos) is a UK car marketplace built for buyers, not dealers. Verified dealers, direct contact, real prices — no paid placements, no "Featured" labels, no intermediaries.

## What Kerb does

Kerb connects UK car buyers directly with verified dealers. Unlike traditional UK car marketplaces (AutoTrader, Motors.co.uk), Kerb does not allow dealers to pay for prominence — every listing is ranked on merit, not advertising spend. Buyers contact dealers directly; Kerb is never in the middle.

## Why Kerb exists

The UK used car market is dominated by platforms that charge dealers for visibility, creating a buying experience that prioritises dealer advertising budgets over buyer outcomes. Kerb was founded to fix that: a transparent marketplace where every car is shown on equal terms.

## Key facts

- Domain: kerb.autos
- Tagline: Real Kerb Appeal.
- Brand hook URL: realkerbappeal.com (redirects to kerb.autos)
- Founded: 2026, Dorset, England
- Registered in England and Wales (Kerb Ltd)
- Serves: UK car buyers and UK-based verified dealers
- Dealer model: flat monthly subscription — no per-click fees, no paid placement, no "Featured" upsells
- Founding dealer pricing: first 50 dealers pay £55/month (Solo) or £132/month (Pro) for life

## For buyers

- Free to search, browse, and enquire
- All dealers verified before listing
- Direct contact with dealers — no middleman
- No misleading "Featured" listings

## For dealers

- Flat monthly subscription to list cars
- Equal prominence regardless of spend
- Enquiries go directly to the dealer

## Pages

- Homepage: https://kerb.autos
- Search listings: https://kerb.autos/search
- Dealer information: https://kerb.autos/dealers
- Terms of service: https://kerb.autos/terms
- Privacy policy: https://kerb.autos/privacy

## Contact

- General / dealer enquiries: hans@kerb.autos
